channel.title: The Mutapa Times — Zimbabwe Business & Economic Policy
channel.description: Strictly Zimbabwe business, finance, mining, investment and economic-policy stories — curated from foreign press and policy press. Each item ships with a branded headline card for social autolists.

item.title: Zimbabwe’s lithium beneficiation drive triggers energy investment surge
item.description: Zimbabwe’s lithium beneficiation drive triggers energy investment surge — via The Herald ZW #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Herald ZW

item.dc:creator: Business Insider Africa

item.title: Kavango raises Zimbabwe pension fund stake as Nara Gold acquisition advances @nehandaradio
item.description: Kavango Resources plc is increasing Zimbabwean pension fund participation in the company through a new share issuance as it continues efforts to finalise its acquisition of the Nara Gold Project. The post Kavango raises Zimbabwe pension fund stake as Nara Gold acquisition advance #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Nehanda Radio

item.title: Britain’s slice of Zimbabwe’s services market just jumped to 8.2%
item.description: The headline UK services trade with Zimbabwe softened year on year. The structural number underneath it tells the better story: Britain’s share of all services Zimbabwe imports rose 2.4 percentage points to 8.2%, the highest measured level in years. As Zimbabwean buyers re-rate their global supplier mix, the UK is taking a bigger slice of the country’s services demand. #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Tendai Kuwanda

item.title: Zimbabwe’s growth is now hot enough to lift Britain’s exporters: UK goods sales to Harare up 14.6%
item.description: The Zimbabwean economy is in its strongest stretch in years: 7.5% GDP growth in 2025, a projected 5% in 2026, and that growth is pulling demand for cars, commercial vehicles, medicines and industrial machinery in with it. UK goods exports to Zimbabwe rose 14.6% on the year, the strongest line in an otherwise soft bilateral picture for Britain. #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Tendai Kuwanda

item.title: Zimbabwean tobacco still does Britain’s heavy lifting: £129m, and held firm
item.description: Of every £100 of goods Zimbabwe sells to Britain, £96 is tobacco or beverages. While the wider UK–Zimbabwe trade relationship softened in the year to December 2025, Zimbabwean tobacco held its grip, and the IMF projects Zimbabwean GDP growth of 7.5% in 2025 and 5.0% in 2026. The product that built Zimbabwe’s name as an exporter is still doing the work. #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Tendai Kuwanda

item.title: AU Summit opens investment opportunities for Zimbabwe @HeraldZimbabwe
item.description: Nyore Madzianike Senior Reporter THE African Union Mid-Year Summit, set for next month in Egypt, will provide Zimbabwe with an opportunity to expand its investment portfolio as the event will... #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Herald

item.title: Henkel Ventures co-leads Ruka’s $4.5m round
item.description: Ruka, the London-based textured-hair company founded by Zimbabwean entrepreneur Varaidzo Tendai Moyo and her co-founder Ugorji Agbai, has closed a $4.5m round co-led by Henkel Ventures and Freedom Trail Capital. The deal, which takes lifetime funding to roughly $10m and brings one of the world’s largest consumer-goods groups onto the cap table, is the most meaningful endorsement yet of a Zimbabwean diaspora-founded brand. #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Tendai Kuwanda

item.title: ZDAMWU calls for worker‐centred green transition in mining sector @bulawayo24
item.description: The Zimbabwe Diamond and Allied Minerals Workers Union (ZDAMWU) has called for a worker‐centred transition to a green economy, warning that the shift in the mining sector must not come at the expense... #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News

item.title: Malls power new wave of investor confidence in Zim property @zimsituation
item.description: Source: Malls power new wave of investor confidence in Zim property -Newsday Zimbabwe INVESTOR confidence in Zimbabwe’s real estate sector is increasingly anchored on adaptive commercial models that can withstand economic uncertainty, economists have said. Analysts say the shift #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Zimbabwe Situation

item.title: Ministry halts illegal construction at Harare’s protected Ramsar Site @zimsituation
item.description: Source: Ministry halts illegal construction at Harare’s protected Ramsar Site –Newsday Zimbabwe HARARE, May 12, 2026, The hum of a bulldozer is rarely the sound of progress when it echoes across the delicate grasses of Monavale Vlei, one of the few remaining lungs of Harare. To #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Zimbabwe Situation

The post Nurses Carry Our Lives in Their Hands and Must Be Protected, Respected and Empowered appeared first on 263Chat. #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews

item.title: ZSE Defies Liquidity Slowdown as Blue-Chip Counters Lift All-Share Index @ZSE_ZW
item.description: HARARE – The Zimbabwe Stock Exchange delivered a mixed but resilient performance in April 2026, with gains in the benchmark All-Share Index offsetting a steep decline in market turnover and trading volumes as investor activity softened across the bourse. According to The Herald, #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews

item.title: Millennium Heights apartments sell out fast as demand surges
item.description: Millennium Heights is rapidly taking shape, with construction progressing at pace on its planned 1,000 apartments and demand significantly outstripping supply. Block 6, comprising 65 units, sold out before construction had even begun and is now officially under development. Follo #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews

item.description: SMALL to medium enterprises in the leather sector have called for measures to improve access to affordable capital needed to upgrade machinery and enhance product quality to compete with imports. Zimbabwe’s leather sector is undergoing a structural evolution, transitioning from a #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe Mail

item.title: Chiyangwa challenges graduates to create jobs, build economy
item.description: HARARE – Prominent businessman Dr Philip Chiyangwa has urged empowerment graduates to transition from job seekers to employment creators, leveraging their practical skills to stimulate Zimbabwe’s grassroots economy. Speaking as the guest of honour at The Tonganyika Trust graduati #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe Mail

item.title: Digital Twins and the Future of Infrastructure Investment in Zimbabwe
item.description: Why Zimbabwe Can No Longer Afford Inefficient Infrastructure Planning Zimbabwe is entering a period where infrastructure investment decisions will increasingly define the country’s long-term economic trajectory. From roads and railways to energy systems, water infrastructure, and #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe Mail

item.title: Engineering, Construction and Building Materials in Zimbabwe: Building Value in a Transforming Economic Landscape
item.description: A Sector Under Structural Recalibration Zimbabwe’s engineering, construction, and building materials sector is undergoing a profound structural transition shaped by shifting macroeconomic conditions, constrained public investment, rapid urbanisation, and increasing demand for res #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe Mail

item.title: The Case for a Clear ROI Framework for Infrastructure and Transport Projects in Zimbabwe
item.description: Infrastructure Investment at a Fiscal Crossroads Zimbabwe’s transport infrastructure system is standing at a defining moment shaped by fiscal constraints, ageing public assets, rising construction costs, and shifting development priorities. From the National Railways of Zimbabwe #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe Mail

item.title: How AI Is Transforming Strategy Development in Zimbabwean Business: The Emergence of a New Strategic Era
item.description: Artificial intelligence is rapidly redefining how businesses think, compete, and grow across the global economy. What was once regarded as a futuristic technology reserved for Silicon Valley firms and multinational corporations is now steadily becoming part of everyday commercial #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe Mail

item.title: Building High-Performing Organisations in Zimbabwe Through Marketing, Sales and Organisational Capability: The Changing Nature of Business Competition in Zimbabwe
item.description: Zimbabwe’s business environment has become increasingly complex and competitive over the past decade. Companies are no longer operating in markets where simply producing a good product guarantees growth or sustainability. Today, organisations are competing in an economy shaped by #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe Mail

item.title: Zimbabwe to Return Land to White Farmers After Years of Disputes
item.description: After years of controversy, Zimbabwe moves to compensate and return land to white farmers, raising hopes for economic recovery. The post Zimbabwe to Return Land to White Farmers After Years of Disputes appeared first on iHarare News. #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Iharare News

item.description: By Kudzayi Mutisi-There is something extraordinary about Zimbabweans, something the world may never fully understand. Whether Black, white, Indian,... Continue reading \"Economists Are Baffled by Zimbabwe’s Survival Economy\" #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews

item.title: TelOne, NetOne have more staff than bigger rivals. That raises questions @Techzim @NetOneCellular @TelOneZW
item.description: Here is one of the more interesting things hiding in the latest POTRAZ report: NetOne employs more people than Econet. TelOne employs way more people than Liquid. That sounds strange because Econet is clearly the bigger mobile operator, while Liquid carries far more fixed interne #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews

A foreign currency dealer, Chivhima found himself face-to-face wit #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews

item.title: Zimbabwe to return 67 foreign-owned farms seized in land grab
item.description: Bloomberg Zimbabwe said it was returning a number of foreign-owned farms that were seized during a violent land grab more than 25 years ago and pay compensation of $146 million. Agriculture Minister Anxious Masuka told lawmakers that the transfer involved 67 properties. Treasury #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: Govt finalises arrears, debt clearance roadmap @zimsituation
item.description: Source: Govt finalises arrears, debt clearance roadmap – herald Debra Matabvu Senior Reporter ZIMBABWE has finalised a roadmap for its debt and arrears clearance programme and is targeting early next year to begin repaying outstanding obligations to international creditors. This #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews

item.title: Afdis income jumps 51pc as firm rides on demand and volume surge
item.description: African Distillers Limited (Afdis) total income jumped 51 percent to US$7.7 million, on the back of a 56 percent jump in revenue during the year to March 31, 2026. The beverage maker benefited from firm consumer demand, reduced pressure from the illicit alcohol trade and improved #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews

item.title: So should Zimbabweans be proud that our fuel is second, and not the most expensive in Africa, Prof. Mthuli? @zimsituation @TendaiMbofana
item.description: Source: So should Zimbabweans be proud that our fuel is second, and not the most expensive in Africa, Prof. Mthuli? At times, I wonder if we are still sane in Zimbabwe. item.title: Zimbabwe moves to classify coal as ’special’ critical mineral @bulawayo24
item.description: Zimbabwe is preparing to designate metallurgical coal as a “special critical mineral” in a policy shift aimed at attracting new investment into the country’s vast but underutilised coal reserves, amid... #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews

item.title: Zimbabwe Government Slashes Mining Levies, Streamlines Licences
item.description: HARARE – The Government has approved a reduction in mining sector levies, licences, and fees, alongside sweeping regulatory reforms aimed at lowering the cost of doing business and boosting competitiveness. The measures, approved by Cabinet, according to State Media, are part of #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews

item.title: Marketing Analytics and the Illusion of Strategy: Why Zimbabwean Firms Must Rethink Growth

item.title: ZiG’s Future Depends On Purchasing Power, Not Paper Quality, Says Economist @thezimbabwean
item.description: Economist Luxon Zembe has said that long-term confidence in the ZiG will depend on the currency’s ability to retain value, rather than on the recent launch of better-quality banknotes. #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews

item.title: ZITF sees 61% private sector exhibitor turnout
item.description: INDUSTRY and Commerce Minister, Mangaliso Ndlovu has revealed that 61% of exhibitors who made it to the 2026 Zimbabwe International Trade Fair (ZITF) were from the private sector with the remainder being government entities. ZITF is the country’s premier multi-sectorial exhibitio #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews
